rulings.Case Title: KEVIN STUCKART et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 28, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Union Carbide Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

Leave to amend is liberally granted and there is no prejudice to Oxnard Building Materials Inc., the only party that opposed this motion. rulings.Case Title: HARRY CARPENTER et al VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Defendants Aurora Pump Company And Sepco Corporation'S Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action On The Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 28, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Flowserve Corp.¿s joinder in Aurora Pump Company and Speco Corporation¿s motion to dismiss or stay action is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice as required by C.C.P. sections 1005(b) and 1010.6. Plaintiffs¿ objections based on timeliness are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND GENE BENNETT et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 28, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Grundfos CBS, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden under Aguilar and plaintiffs¿ late evidence will not be considered by the court, thus plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Bennett was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s untimeliness objections to the opposition are sustained. Pursuant to C.C.P. section 437c, plaintiffs¿ opposition was due by August 14. Plaintiffs¿ proof of service demonstrates that its opposition was not served until August 17. Plaintiffs failed to demonstrate good cause for the late filing and service of their opposition and all attached papers. (See Hobson v. Raychem Corp. (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 614, 623-625 (disapproved on other grounds in Colmenares v. Braemar Country Club, Inc. (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1019).) Although not making a ruling, the court notes that even if it had been able to consider plaintiffs¿ evidence, summary judgment would be granted because plaintiffs¿ evidence failed to create a triable issue whether Mr. Bennett was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. (Tentative ruling -- to be continued)
